Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Using scientific quotes for preaching

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-07-28 · 3 answers
I have heard devotees use quotes from current scientific discoveries that are in consonance with the vedic understanding.

The problem is that scientists are always changing their views and the quotes devotees use may be outdated or incorrect in a scientist eyes.

So should devotees use modern science quotes to support their arguments or not?
user [38] · 2007-07-28
As a support for Vedic conclusions. This is the idea of Bhaktivedanta Institute.
user [19] · 2007-07-28
yes but these ideas usually change so if we say:
look, science says that we are right!
but maybe in a few years their arguments change and what will you say?
sorry, now they say something else...
user [38] · 2007-07-28
Ill say: "Yes, its only pratyaksa and anumana level, it shouldnt be mistaken as sabda level, the real science which gives a direct experience of supramundane reality."

This preaching is meant for people who accept modernist scientific paradigm - to undermine it and turn their attention to Vedic wisdom, which is ultimately spiritual.
